label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out whether following these instructions brings me back to the starting point. First, the instructions are: 1. Take 3 steps. 2. Take 7 steps. 3. Turn around. 4. Take 10 steps. I need to visualize or simulate this step by step. Let me assume I'm facing a certain direction, say north, and I take 3 steps forward in that direction. Then I take 7 more steps in the same direction (north), so I'm now 3 + 7 = 10 steps north of the starting point. Then I turn around. If I was facing north, turning around means I'm now facing south. Then I take 10 steps in the new direction (south). Since I was 10 steps north of the starting point and now I take 10 steps south, I end up exactly at the starting point.
